skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Guidance for creating distinctive frontend interfaces with deliberate visual direction, typography, layout, motion, and user-facing copy.
skill.summary: Helps AI agents design frontend interfaces that feel specific, polished, and less templated.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic skill containing frontend design guidance and an Apache license file. Static analysis flagged a license URL, but it is not executable network behavior. No prompt injection, scripts, external commands, or unsafe patterns were found.

content.value_statement: Many AI-built interfaces look generic because visual choices are not tied to the product. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward specific design systems, stronger typography, and clearer interface copy.
actual_capabilities: Creates a compact design plan before frontend implementation.
Selects palettes, typefaces, layout concepts, and signature visual elements.
Reviews design ideas against the brief to reduce generic patterns.
Guides responsive UI quality, keyboard focus, and reduced motion support.
Improves interface copy, labels, empty states, errors, and action text.
Encourages screenshot-based critique when the environment supports it.
limitations: Does not provide reusable components, assets, or executable scripts.
Does not replace user research, brand strategy, or accessibility testing.
Depends on the agent's ability to implement the chosen frontend stack.
May need project-specific constraints for brand, content, and audience.

use_cases.title: Create a New Product Interface
use_cases.description: Plan and build a frontend that has a clear visual identity from the first screen.

use_cases.title: Refresh an Existing Screen
use_cases.description: Reshape layout, typography, color, and copy without making the screen feel generic.

use_cases.title: Critique an AI-Generated Design
use_cases.description: Identify default patterns and revise the direction before code quality work begins.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new page or feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to plan a distinctive interface for [product]. Define audience, page goal, palette, type, layout, and signature element.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Generic Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for an existing UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this screen for generic AI design patterns. Propose specific changes to typography, color, structure, motion, and copy.

prompt_templates.title: Build From a Design Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for implementation after planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the frontend from the revised design plan. Preserve the visual system, responsive behavior, accessibility states, and exact interface vocabulary.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Refine a Complete Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for multi-screen product work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Critique this flow as a design lead. Find weak choices, inconsistent language, missing states, and places where the identity breaks.

output_examples.input: Design a landing page for a precision coffee scale.
output_examples.output: A compact design plan with named colors, display and body type choices, layout notes, and one memorable measurement-inspired interaction.

output_examples.input: Make this dashboard look less like a template.
output_examples.output: A critique that names generic patterns, then replaces them with domain-specific structure, tighter hierarchy, better labels, and restrained motion.

output_examples.input: Review the empty states and error messages in my app.
output_examples.output: Plain-language copy recommendations that explain what happened, what the user can do next, and which action labels should stay consistent.

best_practices: Start with a concrete subject, audience, and page goal.
Spend visual boldness on one justified signature element.
Critique the design before and after implementation.
anti_patterns: Using default AI palettes without linking them to the brief.
Adding decorative numbers, dividers, or motion without information value.
Writing clever copy when users need plain direction.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents make more specific frontend design choices before and during implementation.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: The skill guides design decisions. The agent still writes code using the project's normal tools.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing interface?
faq.answer: Yes. It can critique generic patterns and suggest stronger choices for layout, type, color, motion, and copy.

faq.question: Is it only for landing pages?
faq.answer: No. It can support product screens, dashboards, tools, websites, and multi-screen flows.

faq.question: Does it include design assets?
faq.answer: No. It provides design process guidance, not images, icons, components, or templates.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
